channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1018)—Answers for Wednesday, March 25
item.content:encoded:
How is your week going? Are you keeping that NYT Connections streak running? If you are, let me know in the comments below what number you’re up to by now. On your way down, make sure to stop by the NYT Connections hints and solutions for today’s puzzle. I’ve got the answers you need, and they’re just seconds away.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (March 25)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • WIRE
  • QUARK
  • CLOUD
  • PING
  • SPIN
  • OBSCURE
  • CHARGE
  • FORTUNE
  • CHECK
  • TIME
  • GALLOP
  • BLUR
  • MUDDY
  • PEOPLE
  • CASH
  • CUR

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you might associate with hiding something
  • Green – Names of popular magazines
  • Blue – Ways to pay people
  • Purple – Almost units of volume, just one letter away

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, March 25:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, March 25 (#1018)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BLUR, CLOUD, MUDDY, OBSCURE
  • Green – FORTUNE, PEOPLE, SPIN, TIME
  • Blue – CASH, CHARGE, CHECK, WIRE
  • Purple – CUR, GALLOP, PING, QUARK

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Dis

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: League of Legends Patch 26.7 Preview: Farming Supports Return?
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has given a preview of what League of Legends players can expect from Patch 26.7.

The upcoming update will bring new LoL April Fool skins and some interesting meta changes following First Stand, the first international esports tournament of the season.

It appears Riot wants to enable more AP jungle and AD mid combos as well as farming supports, despite the new role quests.

Here is everything Riot Games is changing in the MOBA as previewed by lead gameplay designer Matt ‘Phroxzon’ Leung-Harrison.

Phroxzon spoke about the current esports meta for LoL and said he was happy with what he saw at First Stand:

“The First Stand meta ended up being quite good; there was significant diversity in every role between classes…

“It really did seem like every lane could carry and balance of objectives vs splitting felt pretty appropriate.”

Phroxzon also highlighted that the role quests have stopped lane swaps and was glad to see that it allowed elite pro players like toplaner Chen ‘Bin’ Ze-Bin to shine.

Though he did also note that there was a lack of teams pivoting to AP junglers and AD mids, a common pairing to keep team comps balanced, and wants to help boost both roles.

The gameplay designer was particularly pleased with the bot lane meta too, claiming there was a good mix of Casters, Hypercarries and Utility as well as fringe picks like Zeri and Kog’Maw due to Fearless Draft.

Phroxzon did admit that ranged supports have began to take over the meta due to a focus from players on completing role quests but claims this is “Not necessarily a bad thing, given that we’ve been in a Melee support meta for many years.”

League of Legends Patch 26.7 Champion Buffs And Nerfs

Champion Buffs

  • Cassiopeia
  • Kalista
  • Rell
    • [E] Full Tilt bonus Move Speed increased 10% (increased to 25% when facing empowered ally or visible enemy) -> 15% (increased to 30% when facing empowered ally or visible enemy)
    • [R] Magnet Storm damage per tick increased 15/25/35 (13.75% AP) 18.75/31.25/43.75 (+13.75% AP)

Champion Nerfs

  • Graves
  • Karma
  • Nami
    • [W] Ebb and Flow bounce effectiveness ratio adjusted -10% (+10% per 100 AP) -> -20% (+15% per 100 AP)
  • Ornn
    • [P-Temper] Living Forge – Temper Brittle target’s max HP damage reduced 10-18.94% -> 9-16.82% (based on Ornn’s level 1-20, linear)
  • Singed
  • Veigar
    • [R] Primordial Burst cooldown increased 100/80/60 120/90/60 seconds

Champion Adjustments

  • Shyvana
    • Shyvana has a bit of an unintended interaction with Sylas that will be micropatched tomorrow; essentially, he benefits double from her auto attacks passive (with Sylas’s passive) that makes his attacks do ridiculous damage
    • Riot is still taking time to evaluate role viability for top Shyvana and whether it’s reasonably supportable without destroying Jungle role satisfaction
    • Riot has also been working on some changes to increase the satisfaction of her Passive, reduce MMR skew, increase counterplay and the satisfaction of her E
    • [R-P] Dragon’s Descent – Fury Ultimate Haste bonus adjusted 0.015 Fury per second per 1 Ultimate Haste -> 1% bonus Fury generation from all sources per 1 Ultimate Haste

System Adjustments

As mentioned earlier, Riot is exploring bringing back farming supports by making changes to the Support role quest.

Phroxzon explained the publisher’s thought process, saying:

“A few patches ago, we talked about reducing the negative impact of the support quest to reduce complexity

“We’re going to pull this all the way this patch. This may result in some farming support shenanigans, but then you’re also denying your bot carry their quest progression; we’re giving it a go to see if anything breaks.”

Are you happy with the current LoL meta? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And it seems more Shyvana changes are coming even though Riot released a Shyvana hotfix one day after her VGU.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation Shuts Down Dark Outlaw Games A Year After Its Formation
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation is shutting down one of its first-party studios before it ever put out a game. On Tuesday, Bloomberg’s Jason Schreier reported that PlayStation is closing Dark Outlaw Games.

If you recall, Dark Outlaw Games was formed in March 2025 by former Call of Duty Zombies lead Jason Blundell.

At the time, Blundell said that they had been “working away in the shadows”, but when they had something to talk about, they would step forward.

That something never came, and the studio is gone after only a year. It also becomes the second Blunell studio to be shut down by PlayStation. His prior company, Deviation, was closed in 2024.

  • MORE LAYOFFS: Epic Games Cuts 1,000 Employees In Mass Layoffs

Details on why the studio is being closed are currently unknown.

In addition to Dark Outlaw Games, Schreier reports that PlayStation is making cuts to its mobile development team as well. In total, roughly 50 people are losing their jobs.

What do you think of PlayStation shutting down another studio and holding layoffs?Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other gaming news, read about Epic Games shutting down several popular game modes. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw
